content.text: Now let me look at the key model classes related to filters and the SCOPE filter field.
Now let me see the full context around the SCOPE handling in AbstractJdbcRepository.java

Now I see the full picture. The bug is:

1. In `QueryFilter.java`, the `SCOPE` field only supports `EQUALS` and `NOT_EQUALS` but the feature request says it should also support `IN` and `NOT_IN`.
2. In `AbstractJdbcRepository.java`, the `applyScopeCondition` method only handles `EQUALS` and `NOT_EQUALS` and throws an exception for anything else.

Let me also check how `Enums.fromList` works and how IN and NOT_IN would need to be handled with multiple scopes.
Good, `Enums.fromList` can handle a list of strings, which would be used for `IN` and `NOT_IN` operations. Now let me check the existing tests to understand the test

package io.kestra.core.models;

import java.util.List;
import java.util.Set;
import java.util.stream.Stream;

import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
import org.junit.jupiter.params.ParameterizedTest;
import org.junit.jupiter.params.provider.Arguments;
import org.junit.jupiter.params.provider.MethodSource;

import io.kestra.core.exceptions.InvalidQueryFiltersException;
import io.kestra.core.models.QueryFilter.Field;
import io.kestra.core.models.QueryFilter.Op;
import io.kestra.core.models.QueryFilter.Resource;
import io.kestra.core.models.dashboards.filters.AbstractFilter;
import io.kestra.core.models.dashboards.filters.EqualTo;
import io.kestra.core.models.dashboards.filters.Prefix;
import io.kestra.core.models.dashboards.filters.StartsWith;

import static org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThat;
import static org.junit.Assert.assertThrows;
import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.assertDoesNotThrow;

public class QueryFilterTest {

    @ParameterizedTest
    @MethodSource("validOperationFilters")
    void should_validate_all_operations(QueryFilter filter, Resource resource) {
        assertDoesNotThrow(() -> QueryFilter.validateQueryFilters(List.of(filter), resource));
    }

    @ParameterizedTest
    @MethodSource("invalidOperationFilters")
    void should_fail_to_validate_all_operations(QueryFilter filter, Resource resource) {
        InvalidQueryFiltersException e = assertThrows(
            InvalidQueryFiltersException.class,
            () -> QueryFilter.validateQueryFilters(List.of(filter), resource)
        );
        assertThat(e.getMessage()).contains("Operation");
    }

    static Stream<Arguments> validOperationFilters() {
        return Stream.of(
            buildQueryFiltersForOperations(
                Field.QUERY, Resource.FLOW,
                Set.of(
                    Op.EQUALS,
                    Op.NOT_EQUALS
                )

    @Test
    void toDashboardFilterBuilder_prefix_shouldReturnPrefixFilter() {
        QueryFilter filter = QueryFilter.builder()
            .field(Field.NAMESPACE)
            .operation(Op.PREFIX)
            .value("io.kestra.tests")
            .build();

        enum TestField {
            NAMESPACE
        }
        AbstractFilter<TestField> result = filter.toDashboardFilterBuilder(TestField.NAMESPACE, filter.value());

        assertThat(result).isInstanceOf(Prefix.class);
        Prefix<TestField> prefix = (Prefix<TestField>) result;
        assertThat(prefix.getValue()).isEqualTo("io.kestra.tests");
        assertThat(prefix.getField()).isEqualTo(TestField.NAMESPACE);

    private static Stream<Arguments> buildQueryFiltersForOperations(Field field, Resource resource, Set<Op> operations) {

content.text: Now let me fix the `applyScopeCondition` in `AbstractJdbcRepository.java` to handle `IN` and `NOT_IN` operations:

Now let me understand the current method flow. For IN, the value will be a list of scopes (e.g., `["USER", "SYSTEM"]`), and the result should be "namespace in (all namespaces that match any of the scopes)". For NOT_IN, similarly.

The key insight: 
- `FlowScope.USER` means "not in system namespace" (i.e., `namespace != systemNamespace` or user scoped)
- `FlowScope.SYSTEM` means "is system namespace" (i.e., `namespace == systemNamespace`)

For `IN` with a list, we need to build an OR condition. For `NOT_IN`, we need to negate it.

    @Test
    void toDashboardFilterBuilder_prefix_shouldReturnPrefixFilter() {
        QueryFilter filter = QueryFilter.builder()
            .field(Field.NAMESPACE)
            .operation(Op.PREFIX)
            .value("io.kestra.tests")
            .build();

        enum TestField {
            NAMESPACE
        }
        AbstractFilter<TestField> result = filter.toDashboardFilterBuilder(TestField.NAMESPACE, filter.value());

        assertThat(result).isInstanceOf(Prefix.class);
        Prefix<TestField> prefix = (Prefix<TestField>) result;
        assertThat(prefix.getValue()).isEqualTo("io.kestra.tests");
        assertThat(prefix.getField()).isEqualTo(TestField.NAMESPACE);
    }

    @Test
    void toDashboardFilterBuilder_equals_shouldReturnEqualToFilter() {
        QueryFilter filter = QueryFilter.builder()
            .field(Field.NAMESPACE)
            .operation(Op.EQUALS)
            .value("io.kestra.tests")
            .build();

        enum TestField {
            NAMESPACE
        }
        AbstractFilter<TestField> result = filter.toDashboardFilterBuilder(TestField.NAMESPACE, filter.value());

        assertThat(result).isInstanceOf(EqualTo.class);
    }

    @Test
    void toDashboardFilterBuilder_startsWith_shouldReturnStartsWithFilter() {
        QueryFilter filter = QueryFilter.builder()
            .field(Field.NAMESPACE)
            .operation(Op.STARTS_WITH)
            .value("io.kestra")
            .build();

        enum TestField {
            NAMESPACE
        }
        AbstractFilter<TestField> result = filter.toDashboardFilterBuilder(TestField.NAMESPACE, filter.value());

        assertThat(result).isInstanceOf(StartsWith.class);
    }

    private static Stream<Arguments> buildQueryFiltersForOperations(Field field, Resource resource, Set<Op> operations) {

    @Test
    void toDashboardFilterBuilder_prefix_shouldReturnPrefixFilter() {
        QueryFilter filter = QueryFilter.builder()
            .field(Field.NAMESPACE)
            .operation(Op.PREFIX)
            .value("io.kestra.tests")
            .build();

        enum TestField {
            NAMESPACE
        }
        AbstractFilter<TestField> result = filter.toDashboardFilterBuilder(TestField.NAMESPACE, filter.value());

        assertThat(result).isInstanceOf(Prefix.class);
        Prefix<TestField> prefix = (Prefix<TestField>) result;
        assertThat(prefix.getValue()).isEqualTo("io.kestra.tests");
        assertThat(prefix.getField()).isEqualTo(TestField.NAMESPACE);
    }

    @Test
    void toDashboardFilterBuilder_equals_shouldReturnEqualToFilter() {
        QueryFilter filter = QueryFilter.builder()
            .field(Field.NAMESPACE)
            .operation(Op.EQUALS)
            .value("io.kestra.tests")
            .build();

        enum TestField {
            NAMESPACE
        }
        AbstractFilter<TestField> result = filter.toDashboardFilterBuilder(TestField.NAMESPACE, filter.value());

        assertThat(result).isInstanceOf(EqualTo.class);
    }

    @Test
    void toDashboardFilterBuilder_startsWith_shouldReturnStartsWithFilter() {
        QueryFilter filter = QueryFilter.builder()
            .field(Field.NAMESPACE)

    }

            buildQueryFiltersForOperations(
                Field.NAMESPACE, Resource.APP,
                Set.of(
                    Op.GREATER_THAN,
                    Op.LESS_THAN,
                    Op.GREATER_THAN_OR_EQUAL_TO,
                    Op.LESS_THAN_OR_EQUAL_TO
                )
            ),

            buildQueryFiltersForOperations(
                Field.FLOW_ID, Resource.APP,
                Set.of(
                    Op.GREATER_THAN,
                    Op.LESS_THAN,
                    Op.GREATER_THAN_OR_EQUAL_TO,
                    Op.LESS_THAN_OR_EQUAL_TO
                )
            )
        ).flatMap(s -> s);
    }

    private static Stream<Arguments> buildQueryFiltersForOperations(Field field, Resource resource, Set<Op> operations) {
        return operations.stream().map(operation -> Arguments.of(QueryFilter.builder().field(field).operation(operation).build(), resource));
    }

}

        }
    }

512	shadowJar {

519	distZip.dependsOn shadowJar

529	def executableDir = layout.buildDirectory.dir("executable")
530	def executable = layout.buildDirectory.file("executable/${project.name}-${project.version}").get().asFile
531	
532	tasks.register('writeExecutableJar') {
533	    group = "build"
534	    description = "Write an executable jar from shadow jar"
535	    dependsOn = [shadowJar]
536	
537	    final shadowJarFile =  tasks.shadowJar.outputs.files.singleFile
538	    inputs.file shadowJarFile
539	    outputs.file executable
540	    outputs.cacheIf { true }
541	
542	    doFirst {
543	        executableDir.get().asFile.mkdirs()
544	    }
545	
546	    doLast {
547	        executable.setBytes(shadowJarFile.readBytes())
548	        ByteArrayOutputStream executableBytes = new ByteArrayOutputStream()
549	        executableBytes.write("\n: <<END_OF_KESTRA_SELFRUN\r\n".getBytes())
550	        executableBytes.write(file("gradle/jar/selfrun.bat").readBytes())
551	        executableBytes.write("\r\n".getBytes())
552	        executableBytes.write("END_OF_KESTRA_SELFRUN\r\n\n".getBytes())
553	        executableBytes.write(file("gradle/jar/selfrun.sh").readBytes())
554	        ZipPrefixer.applyPrefixBytesToZip(executable.toPath(), executableBytes.toByteArray())

559	tasks.register('executableJar', Zip) {

586	/**********************************************************************************************************************\
587	 * Publish
588	 **********************************************************************************************************************/
589	subprojects {subProject ->
590	
591	    if (subProject.name != 'jmh-benchmarks' && subProject.name != rootProject.name) {
592	        apply plugin: 'signing'
593	        apply plugin: "com.vanniktech.maven.publish"
594	
595	        javadoc {
596	            options {
597	                locale = 'en_US'
598	                encoding = 'UTF-8'
599	                addStringOption("Xdoclint:none", "-quiet")
600	            }
601	        }
602	
603	        tasks.register('sourcesJar', Jar) {
604	            dependsOn = [':core:copyGradleProperties']
605	            dependsOn = [':ui:assembleFrontend']
606	            archiveClassifier.set('sources')
607	            from sourceSets.main.allSource
608	        }
609	        sourcesJar.dependsOn ':core:copyGradleProperties'
610	        sourcesJar.dependsOn ':ui:assembleFrontend'
611	
612	        tasks.register('javadocJar', Jar) {
613	            archiveClassifier.set('javadoc')
614	            from javadoc
615	        }
616	
617	        tasks.register('testsJar', Jar) {
618	            group = 'build'
619	            description = 'Build the tests jar'
620	
621	            archiveClassifier.set('tests')
622	            if (sourceSets.matching { it.name == 'test'}) {
623	                from sourceSets.named('test').get().output
624	            }
625	        }
626	
627	        //These modules should not be published
628	        def unpublishedModules = ["jdbc-mysql", "jdbc-postgres", "webserver"]
629	        if (subProject.name in unpublishedModules){
630	            return
631	        }
632	
633	        mavenPublishing {
634	            publishToMavenCentral(true)
635	            signAllPublications()
636	
637	            coordinates(
638	                "${rootProject.group}",
639	                subProject.name == "cli" ? rootProject.name : subProject.name,
640	                "${rootProject.version}"
641	            )
642	
643	            pom {
644	                name        = project.name
645	                description = "${project.group}:${project.name}:${rootProject.version}"
646	                url         = "https://github.com/kestra-io/${rootProject.name}"
647	
648	                licenses {
649	                    license {
650	                        name = "The Apache License, Version 2.0"
651	                        url  = "http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.txt"
652	                    }
653	                }
654	                developers {
655	                    developer {
656	                        id    = "tchiotludo"
657	                        name  = "Ludovic Dehon"
658	                        email = "ldehon@kestra.io"
659	                    }
660	                }
661	                scm {
662	                    connection = 'scm:git:'
663	                    url = "https://github.com/kestra-io/${rootProject.name}"
664	                }
665	            }
666	        }
667	
668	        afterEvaluate {
669	            publishing {
670	                publications {
671	                    withType(MavenPublication).configureEach { publication ->
672	
673	                        if (subProject.name == "platform") {
674	                            // Clear all artifacts except the BOM
675	                            publication.artifacts.clear()
676	                        }
677	                    }
678	                }
679	            }
680	        }
681	
682	        if (subProject.name == 'cli') {
683	
684	            /* Make sure the special publication is wired *after* every plugin */
685	            subProject.afterEvaluate {
686	                /* 1. Remove the default java component so Gradle stops expecting
687	                       the standard cli-*.jar, sources, javadoc, etc.           */
688	                components.removeAll { it.name == "java" }
689	
690	                /* 2. Replace the publication’s artifacts with shadow + exec */
691	                publishing.publications.withType(MavenPublication).configureEach { pub ->
692	                    pub.artifacts.clear()
693	
694	                    // main shadow JAR built at root
695	                    pub.artifact(rootProject.tasks.named("shadowJar").get()) {
696	                        extension = "jar"
697	                    }
698	
699	                    // executable ZIP built at root
700	                    pub.artifact(rootProject.tasks.named("executableJar").get().archiveFile) {
701	                        classifier = "exec"
702	                        extension  = "zip"
703	                    }
704	                    pub.artifact(tasks.named("sourcesJar").get())
705	                    pub.artifact(tasks.named("javadocJar").get())
706	
707	                }
708	
709	                /* 3. Disable Gradle-module metadata for this publication to
710	                       avoid the “artifact removed from java component” error. */
711	                tasks.withType(GenerateModuleMetadata).configureEach { it.enabled = false }
712	
713	                /* 4. Make every publish task in :cli wait for the two artifacts */
714	                tasks.matching { it.name.startsWith("publish") }.configureEach {
715	                    dependsOn rootProject.tasks.named("shadowJar")
716	                    dependsOn rootProject.tasks.named("executableJar")
717	                }
718	            }
719	        }
720	
721	        if (subProject.name != 'platform' && subProject.name != 'cli') {
722	            // only if a test source set actually exists (avoids empty artifacts)
723	            def hasTests = subProject.extensions.findByName('sourceSets')?.findByName('test') != null
724	
725	            if (hasTests) {
726	                // wire the artifact onto every Maven publication of this subproject
727	                publishing {
728	                    publications {
729	                        withType(MavenPublication).configureEach { pub ->
730	                            // keep the normal java component + sources/javadoc already configured
731	                            pub.artifact(subProject.tasks.named('testsJar').get())
732	                        }
733	                    }
734	                }
735	
736	                // make sure publish tasks build the tests jar first
737	                tasks.matching { it.name.startsWith('publish') }.configureEach {
738	                    dependsOn subProject.tasks.named('testsJar')
739	                }
740	            }
741	        }
742	    }
743	}

746

751	    preCommitText = 'chore(version):'
752	    preTagCommitMessage = 'update to version'
753	    tagCommitMessage = 'tag version'

763	    failOnSnapshotDependencies = providers.gradleProperty("release.failOnSnapshotDependencies")
764	            .map(val -> Boolean.parseBoolean(val))
765	            .getOrElse(true)
766	
767	    pushReleaseVersionBranch = providers.gradleProperty("release.pushReleaseVersionBranch")
768	            .getOrElse(null)
769	}
